summary: Backend-only verification of business_date feature + refund-exclusion fix that resolves the 75,000 IQD discrepancy. Created /app/backend/tests/test_business_date_iter166.py with 15 tests covering the migration endpoint (auth + idempotency), business_date stamping on expense creation, business_date filtering in expenses/shifts/break-even/cash-register-closing endpoints, ShiftResponse business_date field, and refund-exclusion in closed-shift total_expenses. Result: 14 PASSED, 1 SKIPPED (no open shift on first branch). Migration endpoint is idempotent — second run returns 0 updates for shifts/orders/expenses (confirmed via response stats). New expenses created via POST /api/expenses correctly stamp business_date inherited from active shift (or current Iraq date fallback). GET /api/expenses excludes category='refund' by default. GET /api/shifts supports new date_from/date_to and date filters via business_date with started_at fallback. Migration response shows shift_expenses_recomputed=32 confirming all closed shifts were recomputed to exclude refund category. No refund expenses currently exist in DB so end-to-end 75K reproduction wasn't run, but every code path that aggregates total_expenses now has category!={'$ne':'refund'} filter (verified at shifts_routes.py lines 448, 761, 977, 1085; server.py lines 538, 2998, 13150).

minor.issue: OrderResponse Pydantic model in server.py (line 1406-1438) does NOT declare a 'business_date' field. Because model_config has extra='ignore', the business_date stored in DB is stripped from the API response. Orders DO have business_date in MongoDB (verified directly: 70/70 orders carry business_date), but client code reading /api/orders responses cannot see it. Reports that read DB directly (cash-register-closing, break-even) work correctly; only the order-list/detail API responses are affected.

server.py line 1455-1483 ShiftResponse model is missing 'business_date'. The shifts_routes.py copy (line 74) DOES include it and is the active model for /api/shifts router calls (verified — test_shifts_list_includes_business_date PASSED). This is just dead-code drift in server.py that should be reconciled to avoid future divergence.

action_items: Add `business_date: Optional[str] = None` to OrderResponse in server.py (line 1406-1438) so frontend / clients can read the order's business_date.
Reconcile/remove the duplicate ShiftResponse in server.py (lines 1455-1483) which is missing business_date — keep only the canonical one in routes/shifts_routes.py to prevent drift.

Optional: seed/test scenario with an actual refund expense + close-shift flow on a staging tenant to end-to-end validate the 75K fix (current DB has 0 refund expenses so we relied on code-path inspection + migration's shift_expenses_recomputed=32 stat).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py auto_migrate_business_dates (line 457-563): Idempotent and safe — only updates rows where business_date does not exist. The shift_expenses_recomputed loop (line 530+) correctly excludes category='refund' when re-summing closed shifts' total_expenses. Verified: 32 historical closed shifts were recomputed on startup.

server.py POST /api/admin/migrate-business-dates (line 19150): Manual idempotent migration. Returns response under 'stats' key (not flat). Auth check allows admin/super_admin/manager — UNAUTHENTICATED returns 401 (verified).

server.py POST /api/expenses (line 2962): Correctly resolves business_date via _resolve_business_date(tenant, branch) before insert. Verified end-to-end: created expense returned business_date matching open shift's business_date.

server.py POST /api/expenses GET handler (line 2985-3030): Default category filter excludes 'refund'. Date-range filter uses business_date with $or fallback to legacy 'date' field — robust for legacy records.
server.py break-even endpoints (line 12136, 12348): Use business_date with $or fallback to created_at — correctly handles cross-midnight shifts.
server.py cash-register-closing (line 13098-13160): Uses business_date for both shifts and expenses with proper fallbacks. Refund category explicitly excluded at line 13150.
shifts_routes.py shift creation paths (lines 111, 245, 308, 400, 619): All four shift-open endpoints stamp business_date=iraq_date_from_utc() at insert time.
shifts_routes.py expense aggregations (lines 448, 761, 977, 1085): All four sites that sum shift expenses now include category={'$ne':'refund'} filter.
shifts_routes.py GET /api/shifts (line 514-566): New date/date_from/date_to query params correctly use business_date with started_at fallback for legacy records.
payroll_routes.py (lines 117, 295, 124, 302): Both deduction and bonus creation resolve and stamp business_date via shared resolve_business_date helper.

shared.py resolve_business_date (line 197-218): Mirrors server.py _resolve_business_date logic — both pull from open shift's business_date or fallback to iraq_date_from_utc(started_at) or current Iraq date. Consistent.

MINOR DRIFT: OrderResponse and one of the two ShiftResponse models in server.py do not declare business_date, so it is silently dropped from those API responses (extra='ignore'). DB has the field correctly.

success_rate.backend: 93% (14 passed / 15 total; 1 skipped due to no open shift on test branch)

test_credentials.super_admin: owner@maestroegp.com / owner123 (not used in this run)

seed_data_creation: Two TEST_-prefixed expenses were created for verification (TEST_business_date_* and TEST_inherit_*). They are tagged with the active business_date and category='other'. Safe to leave; they can be deleted manually if desired.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All backend business_date code paths are verified working (14/15 PASS). Two minor non-blocking issues remain: (1) OrderResponse missing business_date field — DB has it but API strips it (line 1406 server.py); (2) Duplicate ShiftResponse in server.py is missing business_date but is unused (the active one is in shifts_routes.py line 74). Migration is fully idempotent — re-running the POST /api/admin/migrate-business-dates returns 0 updates for shifts/orders/expenses/advances/deductions/bonuses (already migrated by startup hook). DB currently contains 0 refund-category expenses so end-to-end 75K reproduction not executed; rely on code-path review + shift_expenses_recomputed=32 stat from initial migration.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: Pre-fix, shifts that crossed midnight had their financial records (orders/expenses) keyed by created_at-day, while reports queried by closing date. A refund expense (category='refund') was being summed into total_expenses when closing a shift, inflating it by the refund amount (75,000 IQD).
rca_of_the_issue.fix_summary: Added business_date field (Iraq-local YYYY-MM-DD of the shift's started_at) to shifts on open. New financial records (orders/expenses/advances/deductions/bonuses) inherit business_date from the active shift via _resolve_business_date / resolve_business_date helpers. All report endpoints now filter by business_date with $or fallback to legacy created_at/date for old records. Auto-migration (idempotent) stamps business_date on historical records on startup. Shift close + report aggregations exclude category='refund' from total_expenses (5 sites updated).
rca_of_the_issue.verification: 14/15 pytest tests PASS. POST /api/expenses returns business_date inherited from active shift. Migration returns shift_expenses_recomputed=32 then 0 on re-run. category='refund' is excluded from /api/expenses defaults and from cash-register-closing aggregations.
